page: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, providing 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and includes a 54 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but features a high baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the m6in.4xlarge consistently in the average tier across most standard workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1655.0 and a multi-core score of 9804.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 21026.46. In memory-intensive operations, the instance records a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 834.23 GB/sec and an uncached large block write bandwidth of 57.65 GB/sec. For database and key-value store workloads, it processes 15.37 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 7761.73 on PassMark database operations.

A notable performance tradeoff is observed in LLM inference workloads. While prompt processing for a small 135M model reaches an average speed of 1313.36 tokens/sec, prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to a weak tier at 31.78 tokens/sec. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on EBS, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads unless optimized. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, making it a stable, predictable choice for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating at 3.5 GHz under the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance provides 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage. Benchmark results place the instance in the average performance tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9804.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.37 million operations per second. A key tradeoff is its weak prompt processing performance for medium 7B LLM models, though it maintains average speeds for smaller models. The balanced 4.0 GB memory-per-core ratio and high network throughput make this instance qualitatively cost-effective for general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, and standard database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering stable performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high network bandwidth, and optimized integration for stable enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Optimized for network-attached EBS storage with no local drives
Delivers average-tier performance across database and web serving benchmarks
Suited for general-purpose applications, software compilation, and static web serving
